items.why_it_matters: A user who names a style direction is usually much easier to route into a useful next step than someone who only says they want pretty nails.
items.echoer_action: Acknowledge the style direction and keep gathering timing plus removal facts.

items.why_it_matters: Timing is one of the clearest signs that a nail inquiry is moving toward action.
items.echoer_action: Keep the reply warm and direct the next step toward booking facts.

items.why_it_matters: Removal changes appointment length and routing, so it is an important conversion detail rather than a small side note.
items.echoer_action: Acknowledge the detail and keep narrowing time plus style.

items.why_it_matters: First-time uncertainty means the reply should optimize for clarity and low pressure, not for upsell speed.
items.echoer_action: Use calm language and guide toward a simple style filter.

items.why_it_matters: Event-driven nail requests often convert quickly if slot and style fit are handled well.
items.echoer_action: Acknowledge urgency and ask for event date plus available slot.

items.why_it_matters: When the concern is personal and exposed in public, the response must protect dignity while staying useful.
items.echoer_action: Reply gently, avoid echoing the shame language, and guide toward a safer next step.
